title: Lab 28: SELinux Custom Document Root
description: Serve an existing custom document root on a non-default port with SELinux enforcing.

tasks: Configure Apache on clientvm to serve content from /srv/lab28/site on TCP port 8088.
Keep SELinux enforcing, configure the correct file context and port label, open the firewall permanently, and enable the service at boot.
hints: This lab needs both a file-context change and a port-label change.
The provided index file must stay in place.
checks: semanage port -l | grep -Eq '^http_port_t\b.*\b8088\b' && matchpathcon /srv/lab28/site/index.html | grep -Fq httpd_sys_content_t
systemctl is-enabled httpd | grep -qx enabled && firewall-cmd --list-ports | grep -Eq '(^| )8088/tcp($| )'
solution_outline: Add an Apache drop-in for the custom root and port.
Set the SELinux file context and http_port_t label, then open the firewall.
Enable and start httpd without replacing the provided content.
task_titles: Serve the custom document root on port 8088
Apply the required SELinux and firewall changes
solution_commands:
semanage fcontext -a -t httpd_sys_content_t '/srv/lab28/site(/.*)?'
restorecon -RF /srv/lab28/site
semanage port -a -t http_port_t -p tcp 8088
firewall-cmd --permanent --add-port=8088/tcp
firewall-cmd --reload
